title: chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum
intro: Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum, commonly known as garlic chrysanthemum or crown daisy, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects, which help to relieve respiratory issues and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum has many benefits, such as its ability to improve respiratory health by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of asthma, allergies, and bronchitis. Its antiseptic properties can also aid in wound healing, preventing infections, and promoting the recovery of surgical wounds. Additionally, the plant has been shown to possess anti-cancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and reducing the risk of certain types of cancer. By incorporating Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum into one's life, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and enhanced mental clarity, ultimately leading to a more fulfilling and vibrant life.
actions: Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reducing joint pain and swelling, and improving mobility. Its antioxidant properties also contribute to protecting against cell damage, reducing the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and heart disease, and promoting overall well-being. The plant's antiseptic properties aid in wound healing, preventing infections, and promoting faster recovery, while its antimicrobial properties help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi, reducing the risk of infections and diseases. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties help relieve muscle cramps, spasms, and menstrual cramps, improving sleep quality and overall quality of life.
constituents: Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer. The plant's sesquiterpenes, such as artemorin and chrysanthemumol, have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making them effective in treating infections and improving immune function. By harnessing the medicinal power of Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum, people can benefit from improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune response, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
parts: Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum has many medicinal parts, such as its flowers, leaves, and roots. The flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and essential oils, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots of Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have antibacterial and antifungal properties.
preparations: Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum has many herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used for various medicinal purposes. Teas made from the flowers of Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum are often used to lower body temperature and alleviate fever, while also acting as a diuretic to help with water retention. The tincture, which is typically made by steeping the flowers in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin, is used to treat conditions such as headaches and insomnia due to its sedative properties. Infusions of the plant's leaves and flowers are also used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and indigestion.
side_effects: Chrysanthemum coronarium var. coronatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and redness. Overconsumption of the plant's tea or infusion can lead to stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. In rare cases, improper use of the plant may also trigger more severe reactions, such as anaphylaxis or respiratory problems in individuals with pre-existing conditions. If not handled and used correctly, the plant may also interact with certain medications, reducing their effectiveness or increasing the risk of adverse interactions.
